title: Uniform Prior: No Free Lunch in Exploration

runtime.expected_outcome: With a uniform D prior over the 11x11 maze, early exploration is necessary; a peaked D cuts this cost dramatically.

symbols.D: prior over the initial state (D vector in the bundle)

audiences.kid: If you start with no idea where you are, you have to look around a lot. If you already have a guess, you can go faster.
audiences.real: The D vector encodes prior belief about the start state. A uniform D forces wide exploration; a peaked D concentrates policy near the informed start.
audiences.equation: BundleBuilder default: D_i = 1 at start_idx, 0 elsewhere. Override with uniform D to see the epistemic cost.
audiences.derivation: Initial entropy H[q(s_0)] bounds the total info gain required. Under uniform D, the agent must work log N nats of entropy down; under peaked D, zero.

orchestrate.objective: Quantify how prior entropy (D) maps to exploration cost.
orchestrate.context: hierarchical_maze 11x11; toggle D between peaked (default) and uniform.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), App B.
Cover & Thomas (2006), Elements of Information Theory.
